title: Marketing Manager — Corporation
description: You're a Marketing Manager at a large Australian company. You lead a team of 3-5 marketers, manage significant budgets, and work cross-functionally with product, sales, and executive teams. Your role is strategic with layers of approval, but the resources and impact are substantial.

schedule.activity: Review overnight campaign performance dashboards and flag anything unusual
schedule.note: Data review is always first — it sets your priorities for the day

schedule.activity: Team standup — each team member shares progress, blockers, and priorities
schedule.note: Keep it under 15 minutes. Focus on blockers, not status updates.

schedule.activity: Review and approve creative assets for upcoming product launch campaign
schedule.note: Your job is to ensure brand consistency and strategic alignment

schedule.activity: Cross-functional sync with Product team about Q3 feature launches and marketing support needed
Deep work: Writing the quarterly marketing strategy presentation for the CMO
schedule.note: This is your highest-value work — block your calendar for it

schedule.activity: Lunch — often used for informal catch-ups with Sales or Product leads
Budget review meeting with Finance — justifying marketing spend and forecasting Q3 ROI
schedule.note: Learn to speak the language of finance: ROI, CAC, CLV, pipeline value

schedule.activity: Review agency deliverables — creative concepts for brand campaign, provide feedback
1:1 coaching session with junior marketing coordinator
schedule.note: People management is a major part of this role

schedule.activity: Review A/B test results from email campaign and decide on next iteration
Stakeholder alignment: brief the Sales team on upcoming campaign messaging and materials
Plan tomorrow's priorities, respond to remaining emails, update Asana boards
End of day — occasionally attend industry events or marketing meetups in the evening

responsibilities: Own the marketing strategy and annual marketing plan
Manage and develop a team of 3-5 marketers
Control and justify a six-figure marketing budget
Report marketing performance to the CMO and executive team
Coordinate with Product, Sales, and Customer Success teams
Manage relationships with external agencies and vendors
Ensure brand consistency across all touchpoints
Drive pipeline and revenue through marketing campaigns
challenges: Multiple layers of approval slow down execution
Balancing long-term brand building with short-term performance pressure
Proving marketing ROI to finance and executive stakeholders
Managing a team while still contributing strategically
Navigating corporate politics and competing priorities
Keeping up with marketing technology and industry changes
rewards: Significant budgets mean you can execute ambitious campaigns
Access to data, tools, and resources that smaller companies can't afford
Clear career progression path to Senior Manager, Director, VP, CMO
Working with talented specialists across the marketing mix
Impact at scale — campaigns can reach millions of people
